Example entry.title: Telecommunications
Example entry.description: short description on telecommunications (1 or 2 lines max)

Stephen Hawking’s Office.title: Stephen Hawking’s Office
Stephen Hawking’s Office.description: The entire contents of the last office of Stephen Hawking in Cambridge University, and associated awards, documents, mobility and communications equipment.

BBC Heritage Collection.title: BBC Heritage Collection
BBC Heritage Collection.description: The British Broadcasting Company was established in October 1922 as a private enterprise and was renamed in 1927 as the British Broadcasting Corporation as it moved to a public service model.

Daily Herald Archive.title: Daily Herald Archive
Daily Herald Archive.description: The Daily Herald, established in 1911 as a socialist daily, was once the world’s largest selling newspaper. The Daily Herald Archive contains 1,000s of photographic prints and negatives created by the newspaper's in house picture library to illustrate and accompany articles in the newspaper.

The Worshipful Company of Clockmakers.title: The Worshipful Company of Clockmakers
The Worshipful Company of Clockmakers.description: The Worshipful Company of Clockmakers - founded in London by Royal Charter in 1631 - began assembling its collection of clocks, watches and extraordinary objects in 1814, and continues to do so to the present day. This collection forms the basis of the Clockmakers' Museum and includes works by many of the greatest names in horology.

Monotype Corporation Collection.title: Monotype Corporation Collection
Monotype Corporation Collection.description: The Monotype Corporation made the printed word far easier to produce by mechanising the process of casting and composing metal type for printing. The Monotype Collection contains business records, specialised machinery, and the patterns, punches and matrices needed to make type.

related-articles.title: The Monotype Collection: A Brief History

Sir Henry Wellcome's Museum Collection.title: Sir Henry Wellcome's Museum Collection
Sir Henry Wellcome's Museum Collection.description: American-born pharmacist and philanthropist Sir Henry Wellcome assembled over one and a half million historical objects and books relating to the history of medicine from around the world. His medical collection has been on permanent loan to the Science Museum since 1976 and, together with the museum's own medical holdings, forms one of the most significant medical collections in the world.

Kodak Collection.title: Kodak Collection
Kodak Collection.description: Assembled from 1927 onwards, the Kodak Museum Collection contains around 10,000 items of photographic and cine equipment and 50,000 images dating from around 1820 to the present. Many items are displayed in the Kodak Gallery at the National Science and Media Museum in Bradford, which traces the story of photography from the earliest cameras to today's digital images.

Pollock Pipe Collection.title: Pollock Pipe Collection
Pollock Pipe Collection.description: The Manchester clay tobacco pipe manufacturer John Pollock & Co was founded by Edward Pollock in 1879 and operated across three generations of the family. The collection includes clay tobacco pipes, pipe moulds, metal heels and the novelty pipes, such as bubble gun and bird whistle pipes, produced from the 1930s onwards.

Impressions Gallery.title: Impressions Gallery
Impressions Gallery.description: Impressions Gallery is an independent photography gallery that opened in York in 1972 and moved to Bradford in 2006. The National Science and Media Museum holds an internationally significant selection of contemporary photography from the Impressions Gallery archive, accumulated over the gallery's first 40 years and including early work by photographers such as Martin Parr.

Jane and Howard Ricketts Collection.title: Jane and Howard Ricketts Collection
Jane and Howard Ricketts Collection.description: The Jane and Howard Ricketts Collection consists of prints, albums, ephemera and objects assembled by Howard and Jane Ricketts. The collection concentrates mainly on 19th century British photography and is also rich in accompanying documentary materials.

Lancashire Coal Mining Collection.title: Lancashire Coal Mining Collection
Lancashire Coal Mining Collection.description: Transferred from Salford Museum and Art Gallery, the Lancashire Coal Mining Collection includes hand tools, safety equipment, personal items and machinery used by miners of the Lancashire coalfields from the 18th century to the 1980s. It also contains 36 boxes of archive material including NUM branch records, mining disaster ephemera, personal memorabilia, photographs and administrative records of the Lancashire Coal Mining Museum at Buile Hill.

Andor Kraszna Krausz Collection.title: Andor Kraszna Krausz Collection
Andor Kraszna Krausz Collection.description: Born in Szombathely, Hungary in 1904, Andor Kraszna-Krausz came to England in 1937 as a refugee and founded Focal Press in 1938 to publish affordable, practical guides to photography. Donated under the terms of his will, the archive contains his personal papers, travel documents, correspondence, cameras and photographic equipment, alongside files, books and designs relating to Focal Press.

related-articles.title: K is for… Andor Kraszna-Krausz: The influential founder of Focal Press

Roy Ashton & Phil Leakey Collection.title: Roy Ashton & Phil Leakey Collection
Roy Ashton & Phil Leakey Collection.description: Assembled between 1945 and 1980, this collection brings together the work of makeup artists Roy Ashton (1909–95) and Phil Leakey (1908–92), whose pencil sketches, photographs, prosthetics, sculptures, make-up materials and equipment were used primarily on Hammer Films Productions. Leakey was the first makeup artist to receive an on-screen credit for 'Special Make-up Effects'.

National Archive of Railway Oral History.title: National Archive of Railway Oral History
National Archive of Railway Oral History.description: The National Archive of Railway Oral History (NAROH) holds over a thousand hours of recordings capturing the stories of people who worked on Britain's railways. The archive is held at the National Railway Museum in York and was founded and managed by the Friends of the National Railway Museum, supported by the National Lottery Heritage Fund.

related-articles.title: National Archive of Railway Oral History

Zoltan Glass Collection.title: Zoltan Glass Collection
Zoltan Glass Collection.description: Photographer Zoltan Glass began his career as a cartoonist and retoucher in 1925 before moving into photography in the early 1930s and later establishing the Zoltan Glass Studio in Chelsea, London. The collection is an archive of negatives, contacts and ephemera dating from 1930 to 1970, covering pre-war German motor racing at the Nürburgring and Avus circuits, pre-war British advertising, and magazine photography for titles including 'Lilliput' and 'Illustrated'.

Tony Ray-Jones Collection.title: Tony Ray-Jones Collection
Tony Ray-Jones Collection.description: The archive of British photographer Tony Ray-Jones (1941–1972) is held at the National Science and Media Museum and includes approximately 700 black and white prints, 1,700 rolls of 35mm film, 2,700 contact sheets and around 600 boxes of colour transparencies, alongside notebooks, publications, press cuttings, exhibition catalogues and photographic equipment. Projects documented include A Day Off, Old English Customs, Extremists and Gurus.

related-articles.title: By the seaside with Tony Ray-Jones

Silberrad Glassware Collection.title: Silberrad Glassware Collection
Silberrad Glassware Collection.description: The Silberrad Glassware Collection contains over 300 items of laboratory glassware from the Silberrad Research Laboratories, founded by industrial chemist Oswald Silberrad in 1907 at his home in Buckhurst Hill, Essex and from 1933 at Dryads' Hall in Loughton, Essex. The laboratory remained active until Silberrad's death in 1960.

McLachlan Glassware Collection.title: McLachlan Glassware Collection
McLachlan Glassware Collection.description: The McLachlan Glassware Collection contains over 200 items of chemical laboratory glassware from the laboratory of Thomas McLachlan and Partners at 4 Hanway Place, London, which operated until 1973. McLachlan worked as a Public Analyst and Agricultural Analyst, testing animal feedstuffs, fertilizers, drinking water and other consumer products, and also pioneered environmental analysis work in air and water pollution.

Britain's Railways All Change.title: Britain's Railways All Change
Britain's Railways All Change.description: Britain's Railways All Change (BRAC) is a collection of over 170 oral history recordings capturing first-hand recollections of people involved in the UK's railway privatisation process. The interviews, recorded between 2018 and 2022, document the planning and implementation of privatisation and the management of change in the years that followed.

Ronald G. W. Norrish’s Laboratory.title: Ronald G. W. Norrish’s Laboratory
Ronald G. W. Norrish’s Laboratory.description: This collection preserves the contents of the garden shed chemical laboratory in Cambridge belonging to Ronald G. W. Norrish, dating from around 1912. It includes laboratory glassware and equipment such as test tube racks, boiling tubes, flasks, crucibles, tripods and clamps.

Feroze Sarosh Collection.title: Feroze Sarosh Collection
Feroze Sarosh Collection.description: The Feroze Sarosh Collection comprises film technology including cine cameras, projectors and mechanisms dated between 1895 and 1940, assembled by collector Feroze Sarosh.

Martin Richardson Holograms Collection.title: Martin Richardson Holograms Collection
Martin Richardson Holograms Collection.description: The Martin Richardson Collection demonstrates holography as both an art and a science and spans the career of Martin Richardson, who received the first PhD in Display Holography in 1988. The collection tells the story of more than 40 years of holography and includes holograms of figures such as Martin Scorsese, Sir Peter Blake, David Bowie, April Ashley and Will Self.

Thames Collection.title: Thames Collection
Thames Collection.description: The Thames Collection comprises historic television cameras acquired from Thames Television plc, in most cases as complete camera chains including cables and lenses.

EMI Collection.title: EMI Collection
EMI Collection.description: The EMI Collection consists of business ephemera and photographs relating to the company EMI and the development of television.

John Jones Collection.title: John Jones Collection
John Jones Collection.description: The John Jones Collection contains magic lantern slides, including around 2,600 slides across 182 sets originally produced by Riley Brothers of Bradford, assembled by collector John Jones (d. 2010).

Buckingham Movie Museum.title: Buckingham Movie Museum
Buckingham Movie Museum.description: The Buckingham Movie Museum collection was assembled by John Burgoyne Johnson and consists mainly of amateur cine apparatus from the 1920s to the 1980s.

Arthur Frank Collection.title: Arthur Frank Collection
Arthur Frank Collection.description: The Arthur Frank Collection contains photographic lenses and equipment, including items by makers such as Ross and Dallmeyer.
